formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Since the chapter on "Directing" in CBSE Class 12 Business Studies is a management-oriented chapter, it may not have a lot of mathematical formulas. However, I can provide you with some key concepts and laws that can be useful in the context of directing.

Here's a JSON array with the relevant information:

[
  {
    "name": "Law of Unity of Command",
    "formula": "Each subordinate should receive instructions from only one superior",
    "variables": "Subordinate, Superior",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law aims to avoid confusion and overlapping of authority"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Span of Control",
    "formula": "Optimal number of subordinates that can be controlled by a superior",
    "variables": "Span of Control, Optimal Number, Superior",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law emphasizes the importance of maintaining an optimal number of subordinates to ensure effective control"
  },
  {
    "name": "Principle of Scalar Chain",
    "formula": "A line of authority from the highest authority to the lowest authority",
    "variables": "Authority, Scalar Chain",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This principle aims to establish a clear line of authority to facilitate communication and decision-making"
  },
  {
    "name": "Principle of Unity of Direction",
    "formula": "A single goal or objective should be pursued by all departments",
    "variables": "Goal, Objective, Departments",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This principle emphasizes the importance of unity of direction to achieve a common goal"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Subordination",
    "formula": "Each subordinate should be responsible to only one superior",
    "variables": "Subordinate, Superior",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law aims to establish clear lines of authority and avoid confusion"
  },
  {
    "name": "Principle of Line and Staff Authorities",
    "formula": "A clear distinction between line and staff authorities",
    "variables": "Authority, Line, Staff",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This principle emphasizes the importance of maintaining a clear distinction between line and staff authorities to ensure effective decision-making"
  },
  {
    "name": "Principle of Unity of Command",
    "formula": "Each subordinate should receive instructions from only one superior",
    "variables": "Subordinate, Superior",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This principle aims to avoid confusion and overlapping of authority"
  }
]

Please note that these are not mathematical formulas, but rather key concepts and laws related to directing in a business context.
